answer: In 2024, global solar and wind capacity grew by over 20%, surpassing coal in electricity generation in the U.S. for the first time. Solar capacity surged by 29% compared to the previous year. Renewable energy set a new record, hitting 582 GW.

results.title: Solar Surge and Wind Wins: 2024's Renewable Energy Boom ...
results.snippet: Global renewables hit a record 582 GW in 2024. Solar and wind led the way as costs dropped, but barriers still threaten COP28 goals.

results.title: 2024 Trends: Wind and Solar - Orennia
results.snippet: Orennia forecasted 37 GW of U.S. solar growth in 2024 while wind slows to 7 GW, highlighting a shift in renewable momentum.

results.title: Solar and wind surpassed coal in 2024 - Environment America
results.snippet: According to new data from Ember, solar and wind power produced more electricity than coal in the United States for the bulk of 2024.

results.title: Solar power continues to surge in 2024 - Ember
results.snippet: The world is on track to install 29% more solar capacity in 2024 than it did last year, despite unprecedented growth in 2023.

results.title: For the first time ever, wind and solar produced more electricity than ...
results.snippet: But in 2024, solar and wind combined to generate 17% of the nation's electricity—edging out coal, which dropped to just 15%. That's a historic
